summary: This source contains the abstract and references for a 1985 paper by Dorret I. Boomsma and William F. Gabrielli Jr. The abstract argues for the application of behavioral genetic techniques to psychophysiological data to better understand the genetic and environmental contributions to these traits.

principles.statement: Behavioral genetic techniques should be generalized for application to psychophysiological data.
principles.rationale: Because several studies have demonstrated that certain psychophysiological characteristics have heritable components, the same biometrical genetic techniques used for behavioral traits can and should be applied to them.
principles.source_quote: Several studies have shown that certain psychophysiological characteristics appear to have heritable components. Therefore, it would seen appropriate that behavioral genetic techniques should be generalized for application to psychophysiological data.

signature_quotes.text: Therefore, it would seen appropriate that behavioral genetic techniques should be generalized for application to psychophysiological data.
signature_quotes.context: From the abstract, arguing for the integration of behavioral genetics methodology into psychophysiological research.
